tex:
George, Louis and Beatriz have a café.
\newline

(a) George records the number of each type of meal sold.
\newline
He draws a pictogram to show his results.
\newline
All rows are complete except for Salad.
\newline

\begin{tabular}{|l|l|}
\hline
Type of meal & Number of meals \\
\hline
Meat curry & \\
Pasta & \\
Vegetarian & \\
Salad & \\
Fish & \\
Sandwich & \\
\hline
\end{tabular}

Key: = 8 meals

(i) (b) Six salads were sold.
\newline
Complete the pictogram.
\newline

(c) Write down which type of meal was sold most.
\newline

(d) Find the number of meals sold altogether.
\newline

(e) The café also sells drinks.
\newline

\begin{tabular}{|l|l|}
\hline
Drinks & \\
\hline
Cup of tea & \$2.20 \\
Cup of coffee & \$2.80 \\
Bottle of juice & \$1.50 \\
Bottle of water & \$1.35 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}

Johan buys 2 cups of tea, 1 bottle of juice and 1 bottle of water.
\newline
Calculate the change he receives from a \$10 note.
\newline

\$ 

(f) These are the opening times of the café.
\newline

\begin{tabular}{|l|l|}
\hline
Monday to Friday & 8 am to 6 pm \\
Saturday & 9.30 am to 3 pm \\
Sunday & Closed \\
\hline
\end{tabular}

Work out the total number of hours the café is open in one week.
\newline

 hours

(g) One week the café makes a profit of \$1027.
\newline
George, Louis and Beatriz share this profit in the ratio George : Louis : Beatriz = 7 : 4 : 2.
\newline
Calculate the amount of money they each receive.
\newline

George \$ 
Louis \$ 
Beatriz \$ 

(h) In 2019 the rent for the café was \$7275.
\newline
In 2020 the rent is \$7566.
\newline
Calculate the percentage increase in the rent.
\newline

 \%

(i) George drives 315 km from the café to the airport.
\newline
The journey takes 3 hours 30 minutes.
\newline
Calculate his average speed.
\newline

 km/h

stem.content:
George, Louis and Beatriz have a café.
\newline

_rawBody:
George, Louis and Beatriz have a café.

\begin{parts}
\item George records the number of each type of meal sold.
He draws a pictogram to show his results.
All rows are complete except for Salad.

\begin{tabular}{|l|l|}
\hline
Type of meal & Number of meals \\
\hline
Meat curry & \\
Pasta & \\
Vegetarian & \\
Salad & \\
Fish & \\
Sandwich & \\
\hline
\end{tabular}

Key: = 8 meals

\begin{subparts}
\item Six salads were sold.
Complete the pictogram.

\Answerspace[3cm]
\Marks{1}

\item Write down which type of meal was sold most.

\AnswerLine[][]
\Marks{1}

\item Find the number of meals sold altogether.

\AnswerLine[][]
\Marks{1}

\end{subparts}

\item The café also sells drinks.

\begin{tabular}{|l|l|}
\hline
Drinks & \\
\hline
Cup of tea & \$2.20 \\
Cup of coffee & \$2.80 \\
Bottle of juice & \$1.50 \\
Bottle of water & \$1.35 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}

Johan buys 2 cups of tea, 1 bottle of juice and 1 bottle of water.
Calculate the change he receives from a \$10 note.

\$ \AnswerLine[]
\Marks{2}

\item These are the opening times of the café.

\begin{tabular}{|l|l|}
\hline
Monday to Friday & 8 am to 6 pm \\
Saturday & 9.30 am to 3 pm \\
Sunday & Closed \\
\hline
\end{tabular}

Work out the total number of hours the café is open in one week.

\AnswerLine[] hours
\Marks{2}

\item One week the café makes a profit of \$1027.
George, Louis and Beatriz share this profit in the ratio George : Louis : Beatriz = 7 : 4 : 2.
Calculate the amount of money they each receive.

George \$ \AnswerLine[]
Louis \$ \AnswerLine[]
Beatriz \$ \AnswerLine[]
\Marks{3}

\item In 2019 the rent for the café was \$7275.
In 2020 the rent is \$7566.
Calculate the percentage increase in the rent.

\AnswerLine[] \%
\Marks{2}

\item George drives 315 km from the café to the airport.
The journey takes 3 hours 30 minutes.
Calculate his average speed.

\AnswerLine[] km/h
\Marks{1}

\end{parts}

 km

5 & 12 \\

_rawBody:
$y = x^2 - 4x - 3$.

\begin{parts}
\item Complete the table of values for $y = x^2 - 4x - 3$.

\begin{tabular}{|c|c|}
\hline
x & y \\
\hline
-2 & -3 \\
-1 & -6 \\
0 & -3 \\
1 & -6 \\
2 & -3 \\
3 & 0 \\
4 & 5 \\
5 & 12 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}

\Marks{2}

\item On the grid, draw the graph of $y = x^2 - 4x - 3$ for $-2 \leq x \leq 5$.

\Answerspace[6cm]
\Marks{4}

\item Use your graph to solve the equation $x^2 - 4x - 3 = 0$.
x = \AnswerLine[] or x = \AnswerLine[]
\Marks{2}

\end{parts}
